decision.archive_summary: Earlier regression-addition steps are resolved: a repeatable smoke/eval case was added, guarded relation handling was widened to include continue, and harder Chinese multi-topic detour/return cases were added to fixed live evals. Preserve as a compact pin that the solution should avoid whitelist-style hardcoding; the implemented direction was to tighten the packaged guard so it cannot override a shadow-guard veto.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active topic is still the same GC regression/testing thread, so relation=continue. The next turn most likely depends on the recent exchange about whether the previously failing test should now pass and the assistant having run it, so the recent raw turns should stay. Older implementation/progress turns are mostly resolved status updates and are good eviction candidates. The anti-whitelist requirement and resulting guard-policy constraint are still relevant, but can survive as semantic pins rather than raw turns.
